title: Phyang Monastery
tagline: Phyang Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Phyang Monastery, Phyang (or Phiyang) Gompa is a Buddhist monastery located in Fiang village, just 15 or 16 kilometres west of Leh in Ladakh, northern India.
overview.description: Phyang Monastery, Phyang (or Phiyang) Gompa is a Buddhist monastery located in Fiang village, just 15 or 16 kilometres west of Leh in Ladakh, northern India. It was established in 1515.

topPlaces.name: Fiang
topPlaces.description: Phyang (previously known as Fiang) is a village in Leh district of Ladakh in India. It is in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Taroo
topPlaces.description: Taroo is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Phey
topPlaces.description: Phey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Spituk Monastery
topPlaces.description: Spituk Monastery, also known as Spituk Gompa or Pethup Gompa, is a Buddhist monastery in Spituk, Leh district, Ladakh, 8 km from Leh. The site of Spituk was blessed by the Arhat Nyimagung.

topPlaces.name: Shanti Stupa, Ladakh
topPlaces.description: Shanti Stupa is a Buddhist white-domed Stupa (chorten) on a hilltop in Chanspa, Leh district, Ladakh, in north India. It was built in 1991 by Japanese Buddhist monk Gyomyo Nakamura.

topPlaces.name: Zorawar Fort
topPlaces.description: Zorawar Fort is a fort in Leh, Ladakh, India built in 1836 by Zorawar Singh, a general of Dogra ruler Gulab Singh. Zorawar Singh led the Dogra invasion of Ladakh and built the fort at Leh after deposing the last Gyalpo of Ladakh.

topPlaces.name: Kyago Tso
topPlaces.description: Kyagar Tso or Kyagar Lake is a small brackish lake surrounded by mountains situated in the Ladakh region of the northern Indian Union Territory of Ladakh in India. From afar, it can be spotted by its turquoise colour.

topPlaces.name: Sankar Monastery
topPlaces.description: Sankar Monastery, or Sankar Gompa is a Buddhist monastery within an easy half-hour walk from Leh in Ladakh, northern India. It is a daughter-establishment of the Spituk Monastery and the residence of the Abbot of Spituk, the Venerable Kushok Bakula, who is the senior incarnate lama of Ladakh due to his ancient lineage and personal authority.

items.note: Phyang (previously known as Fiang) is a village in Leh district of Ladakh in India. It is in the Leh tehsil.
Taroo is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.
Phey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Spituk Monastery, also known as Spituk Gompa or Pethup Gompa, is a Buddhist monastery in Spituk, Leh district, Ladakh, 8 km from Leh. The site of Spituk was blessed by the Arhat Nyimagung.
Shanti Stupa is a Buddhist white-domed Stupa (chorten) on a hilltop in Chanspa, Leh district, Ladakh, in north India. It was built in 1991 by Japanese Buddhist monk Gyomyo Nakamura.

Zorawar Fort is a fort in Leh, Ladakh, India built in 1836 by Zorawar Singh, a general of Dogra ruler Gulab Singh. Zorawar Singh led the Dogra invasion of Ladakh and built the fort at Leh after deposing the last Gyalpo of Ladakh.

Kyagar Tso or Kyagar Lake is a small brackish lake surrounded by mountains situated in the Ladakh region of the northern Indian Union Territory of Ladakh in India. From afar, it can be spotted by its turquoise colour.

Sankar Monastery, or Sankar Gompa is a Buddhist monastery within an easy half-hour walk from Leh in Ladakh, northern India. It is a daughter-establishment of the Spituk Monastery and the residence of the Abbot of Spituk, the Venerable Kushok Bakula, who is the senior incarnate lama of Ladakh due to his ancient lineage and personal authority.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Phyang Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Phyang Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Phyang Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (8 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (270 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Phyang Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Phyang Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Phyang Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Phyang Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Phyang Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Fiang, Taroo, Phey, Spituk Monastery, Shanti Stupa, Ladakh.
